ai:cl.c: Compresses data into archives, supporting a variety of compression formats.

ai:m.c: Compresses a plain memory area into an empty buffer.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::CompressBuffer(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const int8_t> Input, std::span<int8_t> Output, INT * Result)

ai:i.c: Pointer to the source data.
Pointer to a destination buffer.
The size of the compressed data will be returned in this parameter.

ai:m.c: Add files to a compression object.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::CompressFile(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Location, STRVIEW Path)

ai:i.c: The location of the file(s) to add.
The path that is prefixed to the file name when added to the compression object. May be empty for no path.

ai:m.c: Compresses streamed data into a buffer.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::CompressStream(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const int8_t> Input, FUNCTION Callback, std::span<int8_t> Output)

ai:i.c: Pointer to the source data.
This callback function will be called with a pointer to the compressed data.
Optional. Points to a buffer that will receive the compressed data. Must be equal to or larger than the MinOutputSize field.

ai:m.c: Ends the compression of an open stream.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::CompressStreamEnd(OBJECTPTR Object, FUNCTION Callback, std::span<int8_t> Output)

ai:i.c: Refers to a function that will be called for each compressed block of data.
Optional pointer to a buffer that will receive the compressed data. If not set, the compression object will use its own buffer.

ai:m.c: Decompresses data originating from the CompressBuffer method.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::DecompressBuffer(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const int8_t> Input, std::span<int8_t> Output, INT * Result)

ai:i.c: Pointer to the compressed data.
Pointer to the decompression buffer.
The amount of bytes decompressed will be returned in this parameter.

ai:m.c: Extracts one or more files from a compression object.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::DecompressFile(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Path, STRVIEW Dest, INT Flags)

ai:i.c: The full path name of the file to extract from the archive.
The destination to extract the file to.
Optional flags. Currently unused.

ai:m.c: Decompresses one file to a target object.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::DecompressObject(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Path, OBJECTPTR Object)

ai:i.c: The location of the source file within the archive. If a wildcard is used, the first matching file is extracted.
The target object for the decompressed source data.

ai:m.c: Decompresses streamed data to an output buffer.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::DecompressStream(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const int8_t> Input, FUNCTION Callback, std::span<int8_t> Output)

ai:i.c: Pointer to data to decompress.
Refers to a function that will be called for each decompressed block of information.
Optional pointer to a buffer that will receive the decompressed data. If not set, the compression object will use its own buffer.

ai:m.c: Must be called at the end of the decompression process.

ai:i.c: Refers to a function that will be called for each decompressed block of information.

ai:m.c: Find the first item that matches a given filter.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::Find(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Path, INT CaseSensitive, INT Wildcard, struct CompressedItem ** Item)

ai:i.c: Search for a specific item or items, using wildcards.
Set to true if Path comparisons are case-sensitive.
Set to true if Path uses wildcards.
The discovered item is returned in this parameter, or NULL if the search failed.

ai:m.c: Deletes one or more files from a compression object.

ai:i.c: The full path name of the file to delete from the archive.

ai:m.c: Scan the archive's index of compressed data.
ai:m.p: ERR cmp::Scan(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Folder, STRVIEW Filter, FUNCTION Callback)

ai:i.c: Only items within the specified folder are returned. Use an empty string for files in the root folder.
Search for a specific item or items by name, using wildcards. If empty, all items will be scanned.
This callback function will be called with a pointer to a CompressedItem structure.

ai:f.c: Apply an archive name to the object, allowing it to be used as a named object in the file system.
The compression level to use when compressing data.
Provides feedback during the de/compression process.
Indicates the minimum output buffer size that will be needed during de/compression.
Resulting messages will be sent to the object referred to in this field.
Required if an archive needs an encryption password for access.
Set if the compressed data originates from, or is to be saved to a file source.
Default permissions for decompressed files are defined here.
Indicates the size of the source archive, in bytes.
The total number of bytes that have been output during the compression or decompression of streamed data.
The total decompressed size of all files in an archive.
Special option for certain compression formats.
